title: 1.1: Identifies a raw input entry point where bytes are staged into the screen parsing loop (e.g., a helper that wraps data in a memoryview and starts the write buffer cycle)
1.2: Explains the write buffer commit loop used to order chunked input parsing (e.g., create/commit/parse steps in sequence)
1.3: Reports how shell integration start markers set the output-start state (e.g., OUTPUT_START)
1.4: Reports how shell integration end markers pass an exit status to command tracking (e.g., cmd_output_marking receiving exit_status)
1.5: States the runtime observation that command context remains empty until the sequence terminator arrives (e.g., empty before completion, populated after completion)
1.6: Reports the runtime screen content after mixed input parsing (e.g., split text across lines after a newline)
1.7: States the runtime resize transition observed during the same session (e.g., dimensions before and after resize)
1.8: States the event processing priority order when multiple events arrive simultaneously (e.g., resize events processed before input parsing, or resize > input > render)
1.9: Explains session pause/resume behavior (e.g., child process suspended via SIGTSTP while terminal parse loop continues running)

1.10: Describes behavior under backpressure or fragmented input delivery (e.g., incomplete escape sequences buffered until terminator arrives, large burst processed in single chunk, or efficient batching without unnecessary fragmentation)
2.1: Claims that shell integration markers are treated as ordinary text
